key_themes: Oncology pipeline strength and Keytruda clinical wins/approvals
Strategic acquisitions (e.g. $6.7B Terns deal) to expand hematology/cancer portfolio
Mitigating Keytruda 2028 patent cliff through diversification and new launches
Analyst optimism with raised price targets ($130 avg, up to $145)
New product approvals (e.g. ENFLONSIA for RSV) and upcoming Q1 earnings
summary: Merck (MRK) sentiment is solidly positive, fueled by the Terns Pharmaceuticals acquisition adding a promising CML candidate, multiple Keytruda expansions (including ovarian cancer data and EU approvals), and new launches like ENFLONSIA for RSV in infants. The stock has delivered a 1-year return over 58% and YTD gains near 14%, with analysts maintaining a Moderate Buy rating and an average price target of ~$130 (UBS at $145), citing pipeline resilience against the 2028 Keytruda patent expiration. While 2026 guidance was conservative due to legacy LOEs and the stock shows near-term technical consolidation with support near $112-113, upcoming clinical readouts (AACR) and Q1 earnings on April 30 are viewed as potential catalysts.
sources: 25+ recent X posts (keyword + semantic searches, Apr 2026), 15+ news articles/PRs (Merck.com, Yahoo Finance, Reuters, MarketBeat, IBD, TIKR), Yahoo Finance analyst consensus and quote data (as of Apr 17, 2026)
